Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-91-081
Dear Mr. Rovrney:
You are seeking advice on behalf of the Mariposa Publj-c
Utility District 
(t'MPuDn) Board of Directors ("board") under the
confliLt-of- interest provisions of the PoliticaL Reform Act (the
rr6sg"1.1 we provide you with advice as the board's authorized
representat j,ve, pursuant to Regnrlation 18329 (coPy encLosed) '
The foll,owing advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of February 7, 7991 and a copy of the district's 
fire
proecti-on ordinance subsequently provided to us.
The advice provided in this letter is Iinited to provisions
of the Act. 
Eecause the commission does not provide advice with
respect to past conduct, the following advice is prospective in
its application.2 The advice is provided to you in your
relrelL"tative capacity for the board (an understanding confirmed
wilh your of f j.ce by telephone on l'larch 4, L991-) and does not
addreis potential lonft i.it-of- interest issues concerning other
individual-s.
OUESTTON

prohibited from voting to make the position of fire chief a
compensated one?
CONCLUSION
on the basis of the facts given, board rnembers are not
prohibited from voting to make the position of fire chief a
tornpensated one. The fire chief should seek advice concerning
whether his participation in certain decisj-ons is prohibited under
the Act's confl ict-of-interest 
provisions.
FACTS
Fire protection, water, and sewer services are provided
r.rithin =p"-cifi"a boundaries in Uariposa county by the Mariposa
public ULiIity District. 
To date, the fire department is. staffed
by volunteersi Bill Bondshu, a nember-of the volunteer fire
dlpartment, was appointed to the position of fire chief by the
MPiID directors. 
Subsequently ur. Bondshu suggested, and on
January 25, :-99l the ttpuo diiectors conditionaLly voted, to.make
the poSition of fire chief a compensated one, at a rate of S30o
per ironth without any other district 
enployee. benefits ' 
Th"
-board's vote was contingent upon a deternination that such an
action would not violate the Act and other statutes '
Mr. Bondshu is a salaried employee at his father's insurance
agency. The agency provides tfre lfpUO with liability 
and fidelity
b6nd insurance 
- po licies, offering insurance coverage for the
activities of MPUD servi,ces, j'ncluding fire protection'
ANALYSI S
A public officiaf 
may not nake, participate-in or use his or
her official position to influence a governmentaL decrsron rn
which the official 
knows or has reason to know he or she has a
f inancj.af j-nterest. (Section 87100.) A financial interest in a
a".i"i"" 
exists if it is reasonably ioreseeable that the decision
,iff 
n.r" a naterial financia] effect, distinguishabfe from its
;;;;"a 
on the publ j.c generally, on the officiil 
or a menber of his
or her innediate fauriiy. A flnancial interest is also present if
the decision will have a specified financial effect on a business
"iriiiy in wtrich the officiiL 
invests or hords a.position. of
rnanaqlment, on real- property i-n which the official 
is interested'
;;";;-;-;;;rce 
of in-corni or sift to the official' 
(section
871o3. )
Decision bY boaral Benbers
As public officials 
(Section 82048), mernbers 9f !h? l'lPUD must
aisquaiiiy themselves froru any board of directors decisions which
;iii-h;;;'a 
reasonabry forese-eable naterial financial effect'
ii=ting"j-snable from the pullic generalty, on them or their
interests.
The effect of a decision is reasonably foresg:?!1t if. there
i." . ="utiittti.r lii"iino"a iit"t it will occur' while certaintv


is not required, an effect that is nerely a possibility is not
reasonably foreseeable. (Downev cares v. Dohrnev conmunitv
Developnent Com. (19871 L96 Cal.App.3d 983; In re Thorner (L975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198.) The Act, however, does seek to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. 
(witt v. Morror',
(1977) 7O Cal.App.3d 817, 823.)
You have not provided any facts which suggest that a board
menber's participation in the vote to make the position of fire
chief a compensated one !.ould result in a financiaL effect on the
board menber as provided for in Section 87103. The relationship
of each board member in an official capacity to the insurance
agency and, through the agency to l'{r. Bondshu, is not one to which
any oi the foreseeability and nateriality criteria of Section
azror appear to appty. Therefore, absent additionaf information
to the contrary, we have no basis upon which to conclude that a
board member's participation in the vote to nake the position of
fire chief would run contrary to the Act's conf l ict-of- interest
provisions.
Future decisioas bY l(!. BoDdsbu
whether a particular decision j.n which l{r. Bondshu as MPUD
fire chief participates wouLd trigger application of the Act's
conf l ict-of-interest 
provisions because of hj,s status as an
employee with the agency providing insurance services for the
distrj-ct \.ri11 depend on the facts of the decision in question.
other than noting that one aspect of the Act's foreseeability and
nateriat j,ty criteria references an official's 
source of incorne
(Section 8t103(c)), we cannot provide advice on this question.
when appropriate, we suggest that Mr. Bondshu or his authorj-zed
representative seek advice.
I trust this letter has provided you with the assistance you
have sought. If you have any further guestions regarding this
rnatter please contact me at (916) 322-59OL.
